attributes.action: Fireworks should be regulated and non-regulated fireworks should be banned.
attributes.additional_details:
Its got to be regulated by the government, first.

Fireworks seem to start a week before the Bonfire Night (November 5) and go on through Diwali (November 8), New Year's Eve, and could go on for weeks later.

On records, there HAD been petitions about their noise to be reduced from 120 to 90 decibels.

But that won't resolve fireworks getting in hands of people that use it in residential areas. As well as past mid-night.

And only local councils or regulated organizations / bodies can buy them.
attributes.background: They should be regulated, out of residential areas range, where there can't be elderly, people with hearing issues or even animals / pets that could be rather sensitive to noises and all.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petitions, which call for similar action:

Ban fireworks in residential areas to prevent stress to people and animals
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/749665

Reduce the time of day that fireworks can be set off to between 5pm & 8pm
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/749900
